book_summary: Leviticus is complete: all 27 chapters exist in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book is framed as the continuation of Exodus presence: YHWH calls from the tent and teaches Israel how holiness touches offerings, repair, priestly service, bodies, food, illness, time, land, economics, justice, grief, communal cleansing, Jubilee, covenant accountability, vows, and sacred property. Difficult material is source-preserved with explicit anti-shame, anti-ableism, anti-misogyny, anti-queer-harm, anti-slavery, anti-vigilantism, anti-trauma, and anti-spiritual-abuse safeguards.

chapters.notes: Vow valuations, poverty adjustment, animals, houses, fields, Jubilee calculation, redemption plus fifth, firstborn, devoted things, tithe, and anti-human-worth-ranking safeguards.

translation_notes: Korban/offering is framed as bringing-near, not crude appeasement.
Clean/unclean is ritual-state language, not human-worth ranking or body shame.
Bodies, birth, menstruation, illness, disability, sex, and grief are not treated as moral contamination.
Leviticus 19 anchors holiness in social justice: gleaning, wages, disability justice, honest courts, neighbor love, sojourner love, elder honor, and honest measures.
Leviticus 25 anchors economics in land rest, redemption, anti-interest protection, release, and refusal of crushing rule.
Difficult sexual and penalty texts are preserved with categorical rejection of modern hatred, coercion, persecution, vigilantism, and identity-based violence.
Leviticus 26 ends in covenant remembrance and non-abandonment, not rejection.
Leviticus 27 valuation tables regulate vow obligations; they do not measure human dignity or sacred worth.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Full-book lexical audit needed for korban, olah, minhah, shelamim, chatat, asham, kippur, clean/unclean, holiness, and redemption terminology.
Festival, Jubilee, and covenant-warning material require cross-comparison with Exodus, Numbers, Deuteronomy, later prophets, and Jewish reception traditions.
Sexual-boundary and severe penalty texts require dedicated historical, lexical, and reception-history audits with anti-harm safeguards.
Priestly bodily-wholeness restrictions require disability-justice audit.
Slavery/debt-servitude material requires explicit anti-slavery and labor-history audit.
Leviticus 27 human valuation schedules require economic-history, gender/age, poverty-access, and anti-human-worth-ranking audit.
Leviticus 27:29 devoted-person death language requires deep legal and reception-history audit with categorical anti-violence safeguards.
next_actions: Begin Numbers with chapters 1-4: census, camp arrangement, Levite substitution, and tabernacle transport duties, with anti-militarization and anti-exclusion safeguards.
